source.title: Toggling between food-seeking and self-preservation behaviors via hypothalamic response networks

confidence_rationale: Direct hunger-versus-predator behavioural toggling experiment with cell-type-specific manipulation in the LH-PMd-PAG-AgRP loop. PNAS-tier methods, large effect sizes, and a circuit-level mechanism that maps closely onto MECH-280's prediction: rising metabolic drive raises the effective freeze threshold so the agent transitions from threat-driven avoidance to active foraging. Mapping is one step removed (the substrate paper centres PMd-CCK and AgRP rather than LH-orexin specifically), so confidence is high but not maximal.
failure_signatures: If the freeze-to-foraging transition were a hard switch rather than a graded threshold, alpha_override could be replaced by a binary gate without behavioural cost; this paper documents graded transitions.

Predator presentation acutely inhibits AgRP neurons, so the override is not simply 'hunger wins' -- threat suppresses hunger signal upstream and the override has to overcome that competition. A naive MECH-280 implementation that ignores this competition will under-predict freeze persistence under acute (rather than sustained) threat.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hungry mice toggle from defensive to food-seeking behaviour as caloric need rises; AgRP-pathways to BNST and LH potentiate food seeking under predator duress, while PMd-CCK to PAG suppresses feeding under acute threat.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-280's prediction (override_signal raises theta_freeze_effective so the agent transitions from freeze to forage at a drive_level threshold) is the same behavioural dissociation this paper documents, with the sign of dependence on hunger and the route through hypothalamic-PAG circuitry both matching.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper's primary cell-type focus is AgRP and PMd-CCK rather than LH orexin specifically. MECH-280's named mechanism (orexin/peri-LC GABA -> PAG) is one circuit instance of the broader hypothalamic-PAG arbitration this paper characterises; the paper does not isolate the orexin contribution within that arbitration.
mapping.source_context: Mice; freely-moving food-versus-shelter / predator-exposure paradigms; AAV-mediated cell-type-specific opto/chemogenetics in PMd-CCK and AgRP populations; behavioural readouts (food-zone latency, food intake under predator) and fibre photometry.

confidence_components.notes: Top-tier venue, cell-type-specific manipulations, replicated effect across paradigms. Mapping fidelity discounted because the paper does not isolate orexin specifically. Transfer risk moderate -- mouse-to-REE-substrate is intrinsic, but the behavioural arbitration is conserved across mammalian foraging-under-threat paradigms.
